_doc: Minimal Agent Bootstrap Contract for Ocelot-Social. Any agent reading this file MUST create the workspace structure below if it does not exist. Idempotent: safe to read multiple times.

project.name: ocelot-social
project.description: Free and open-source social network platform — Vue/Nuxt frontend, GraphQL/Apollo backend, Neo4j graph database.

topology.backend/: GraphQL Apollo server, Neo4j driver, email service, file uploads, JWT auth
topology.webapp/: Nuxt.js SSR frontend, Vue components, Vuex store, Apollo client
topology.cypress/: E2E tests with Cucumber/Gherkin feature files

structure._doc: Agent creates these folders on first read. Never deletes. Never overwrites existing content.

documentation_model._doc: Four-layer documentation model. Every agent working in this repo MUST follow this.

workpaper.purpose: Session- and task-scoped working documents. Created at session start, closed at session end.

whitepaper.purpose: Stable architecture and system truth. Written once, updated on structural decisions only.

diary.purpose: Temporal context layer. Chronological decision log — why we decided what. Monthly files, max 10 lines per entry.

memory.purpose: Long-term context store. Persists cross-session knowledge, changes, decisions.

bootstrap_rules.on_entry_point_missing: create READ-AGENT.md with project scan summary
bootstrap_rules.agents_md: create AGENTS.md if missing — bridges AAMS to Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Windsurf and all AGENTS.md-compatible tools
bootstrap_rules._ltm_mode_doc: 'markdown' = ltm-index.md only, zero dependencies, works everywhere. 'vector' = ChromaDB + Python required. Default: 'markdown'. Switch to 'vector' once Python env is set up.

gitignore_patterns._note: WORKING/MEMORY/ is intentionally NOT ignored — ltm-index.md is the shared collaboration artifact. Only binary/runtime artifacts are excluded.

agent_contract._doc: Behavioral contract every agent MUST honor when working in this repository.
on_first_entry: 2. Create all workspace.structure folders if missing
3. Scan repository → write first workpaper with sections: session_goal, repository_inventory (file tree + status), key_findings (from README + docs), open_questions, file_protocol, next_steps
on_session_start: 3. Open or create workpaper for this session
on_session_end: 1. Complete workpaper (file protocol, decisions, next steps)
